expectations.text: SHA-pinned all GitHub Actions references (uses: owner/action@sha # vX.Y.Z format)
expectations.evidence: All three actions in both workflow options use the exact format: `uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2`, `uses: actions/setup-python@a309ff8b426b58ec0e2a45f0f869d46889d02405 # v6.2.0`, `uses: pre-commit/action@2c7b3805fd2a0fd8c1884dcaebf91fc102a13ecd # v3.0.1`

expectations.text: Used live sources (GitHub API or WebSearch) to verify action versions exist
expectations.evidence: Step 2 states: 'SHA for v6.0.2: de0fac2e... Verified via: api.github.com/repos/actions/checkout/releases/latest and /git/refs/tags/v6.0.2'. Step 6 verification log contains explicit WebFetch rows: 'actions/checkout latest is v6.0.2 | WebFetch | api.github.com/repos/actions/checkout/releases/latest | v6.0.2 confirmed (published 2026-01-09)' and equivalent rows for setup-python and pre-commit/action, including SHA lookups via /git/refs/tags/.

expectations.text: Checked for CVEs/security advisories on referenced actions or tools
expectations.evidence: Step 3 includes explicit security advisory checks per tool, e.g. 'Security advisories: None found via WebSearch "cspell-cli CVE security advisory 2025 2026"'. Step 6 verification log contains dedicated WebSearch rows for all 6 pre-commit hooks: 'pre-commit-hooks no security advisories | WebSearch | "pre-commit-hooks CVE security advisory 2025 2026" | No CVEs found for pre-commit-hooks' and equivalents for cspell-cli, markdownlint-cli2, biomejs/pre-commit, gitleaks, and sync-pre-commit-deps. A specific CVE was identified and evaluated: 'markdown-it CVE-2025-7969 is unrelated' and 'CVE-2025-48384 applies to git-the-VCS, not the gitleaks scanner tool'.

expectations.text: Used the decision format for version choices where the project has options
expectations.evidence: Each of the four BEHIND dependencies in Step 3 uses a structured decision block with fields: Risk level, Verified via, What changed, Breaking changes, Migration steps, Security advisories, Recommendation, and ends with 'Your call: Include in this batch or defer?' — for example on gitleaks: 'Risk level: ROUTINE ... Recommendation: Update. Pure maintenance, zero risk. Your call: Include in this batch or defer?'

expectations.text: Included a verification log table at the end
expectations.evidence: Step 6 is titled 'Verification Log' and contains a 4-column markdown table (Claim | Tool | Source | Finding) with 23 rows covering all pre-commit hook version lookups, all GitHub Actions version and SHA lookups, all changelog reads, and all CVE/security advisory searches. Followed by a self-check section: 'Every version sourced from a live lookup? Yes ... SHA fetched for every GitHub Action reference in the planned workflow? Yes — checkout, setup-python, pre-commit/action all have SHAs.'

suggestions: The CVE checks for the three GitHub Actions themselves (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, pre-commit/action) are addressed only implicitly via the SHA-pinning rationale referencing CVE-2025-30066. Adding explicit WebSearch rows for those three in the verification log would close this minor gap.

The report could note whether the SHA values correspond to tag objects or commit objects, since the /git/refs/tags endpoint may return an annotated tag object SHA rather than the underlying commit SHA. This distinction matters for maximum supply-chain pinning security.
eval_feedback.overall: Perfect score across all five assertions. SHA pinning is applied correctly to every action reference in both workflow options. All versions were verified via live GitHub API calls with exact endpoint citations. CVE/security advisory searches were performed for all nine dependencies, including specific CVE callouts and false-positive disambiguations. The structured decision format with 'Your call' prompts was used consistently for all four version-delta options. The verification log table is comprehensive with 23 rows and a self-check section confirming completeness.
